title: [MEDIUM] Enforce start_step before complete_step
description: Deep sweep finding: completeStepTool allows a PENDING step to be marked COMPLETED directly; it only rejects already COMPLETED steps. That bypasses start_step, workStartedAt initialization, context-fetched enforcement, worker CODING status updates, and the intended task lifecycle. Fix complete_step so workers cannot complete a step that was never started, unless an explicit legacy/human path is intentionally documented and tested.
definitionOfDone: complete_step rejects PENDING steps with a clear invalid-state error and only accepts IN_PROGRESS steps by default.
The start_step -> complete_step happy path still works and records startedAt/completedAt, stepsCompleted, modifiedFiles, and notes.
Context/ownership enforcement cannot be bypassed by calling complete_step first on a claimed task.
Any intentionally supported human/legacy bypass is explicit in code, documented, and covered by tests; otherwise no bypass remains.
Regression tests cover direct PENDING complete rejection, normal completion success, and claimed-task context/ownership behavior.

implementationPlan.description: Change completeStepTool validation so PENDING steps are rejected with invalidState('Step','PENDING','IN_PROGRESS') or equivalent clear error. Keep already COMPLETED rejection. Ensure ownership is checked before exposing step state, and consider adding assertContextFetched to complete_step to match start_step for claimed tasks.
implementationPlan.note: Enforced complete_step ordering: claimed-worker calls now run assertWorkerOwns and assertContextFetched before plan/step state checks, and only IN_PROGRESS steps can complete; PENDING now returns INVALID_STATE expected IN_PROGRESS. Updated assertContextFetched error text to name the current tool and passed explicit tool names from start_step/complete_step. Regression tests added for direct PENDING complete and claimed-worker missing-context bypass; red check against old completeStep behavior failed 4 tests as expected. Targeted green: 3 files, 20 passed/166 skipped.

implementationPlan.description: Update nextAction/error guidance if needed so callers are directed to moe.start_step when they attempt to complete a PENDING step. Preserve modifiedFiles/note handling for the valid IN_PROGRESS completion path.
implementationPlan.note: Added explicit PENDING-step guidance: complete_step now throws INVALID_STATE with message telling callers to call moe.start_step before moe.complete_step, while keeping context.expectedState as IN_PROGRESS and including a nextAction hint in error context. Confirmed modifiedFiles/note valid path still passes via start_step -> complete_step lifecycle test. Targeted green: 3 files, 20 passed/166 skipped.

implementationPlan.description: Add/update tests: direct PENDING completion rejects; start_step then complete_step succeeds; completing with wrong worker still rejects; completing without get_context on a claimed task cannot bypass ordering if assertContextFetched is added. Include checks for workStartedAt/startedAt/completedAt behavior.
implementationPlan.note: Added regression coverage for the required lifecycle: direct PENDING complete rejects with INVALID_STATE and moe.start_step guidance; start_step -> complete_step succeeds preserving startedAt/completedAt/workStartedAt/stepsCompleted/modifiedFiles/note; wrong-worker rejection remains covered; claimed worker without get_context cannot complete an already-started step. Also updated existing worker-lastActivity test to include contextFetchedBy for the claimed worker. Red evidence: with old completeStep behavior restored, 4 new tests failed because PENDING/missing-context complete resolved instead of rejecting. Green evidence: targeted vitest = 3 files, 20 passed/166 skipped.

implementationPlan.description: Run packages/moe-daemon npm test and npm run build. Verify error handling text remains stable enough for existing tests or update expectations intentionally.
implementationPlan.note: Ran final validation and adversarial review. Verified diff for complete_step ordering/context enforcement and test coverage; no new file/socket side effects on error paths, ownership check still precedes step-state exposure, and legacy no-worker/no-assignment paths remain preserved by enforcement helpers. Fresh validation: npm run build exited 0; npm test -- --reporter=dot passed 25 files / 463 tests; git diff --check clean except CRLF warnings.

planningNotes.approachesConsidered: Rejected silently auto-starting inside complete_step because it hides ordering mistakes and skips start-step nextAction recommendations. Prefer explicit rejection and clear nextAction guidance to call start_step first.
planningNotes.codebaseInsights: startStep is the only tool that records startedAt, workStartedAt, context-fetched ordering, and worker CODING status. completeStep currently accepts every non-COMPLETED status, making startStep optional in practice.
planningNotes.risks: Some tests or legacy scripts may rely on direct complete_step. If kept, it must be an explicit human-only path, not available to claimed worker calls. Include error handling and test updates.
